landing.heroHeadline: Discover and share the best fly fishing spots
landing.heroSubheadline: Save locations, upload your catches, and explore spots shared by other anglers
landing.heroTagline: Fly fishing is much more than a sport. It's reading the river, interpreting the hatches, choosing the right fly. It's connecting with nature — an art passed down through generations of passionate anglers.
landing.builtByFishers:
Built for fly fishers,
by fly fishers
landing.missionDesc: Fly fishing has a deep history in Italy. From the alpine streams of Valtellina to the spring creeks of Friuli, from Val Camonica to the Tuscan-Emilian Apennines, every river has its soul and its secrets. Flywaters was born to collect this knowledge and make it available to all Italian fly fishers — from beginners to expert tyers. A no-kill community where you can share spots, techniques, patterns and catches with respect for fish and the environment.
landing.discoverSpotsDesc: Find the best Italian rivers and streams for fly fishing. Read reviews from anglers who fished there, discover the species present, the best season and water conditions. From the marble trout of the Sesia to the grayling of the Brenta, every spot has its story.
landing.shareCatchesDesc: Post your fishing photos, tag the spot, indicate the technique and fly used. Contribute to the collective knowledge of the community. Every documented catch is valuable data for all anglers who will come after you.
landing.connectCommunityDesc: Get in touch with fly fishers from all over Italy. Exchange tips on patterns, mayfly and caddis hatches, river conditions. Organize trips, find fishing buddies, learn from the most experienced.
landing.noKillDesc: No-kill is not just a rule — it's respect for the fish and the river. Every marble trout released is a trout that will grow again, that will return upstream, that another angler may encounter. On Flywaters, no-kill is a founding value: every photographed catch returns free to the water. Because the real trophy is not the fish — it's the moment.
landing.step1Desc: Create your profile in seconds. Choose your preferred fishing style — dry fly, nymph, streamer or tenkara — and start exploring right away.
landing.step2Desc: Navigate the interactive map of Italian spots. Filter by water type, target species and community rating. Every spot has photos, reviews and access information.
landing.step3Desc: Post your outings, review the spots you visited and message other anglers. Your experience enriches the entire community.
landing.ctaTitle:
Every river tells a story.
Find yours.

seasons.winterDesc: Snow-covered alpine streams, brown trout and grayling in crystal-clear waters. You'll need warm layers and sturdy waders.
seasons.springDesc: The season of hatches. Rivers in full flow, green meadows and the first dry flies of the year.
seasons.summerDesc: Golden lakes at sunset, long sessions with light gear. The perfect time to explore.
seasons.autumnDesc: Golden foliage along the streams, low and clear waters. The season of spawning char and the last hatches before winter.

seo.defaultTitle: Flywaters — The Italian Fly Fishing Community
seo.defaultDescription: Discover the best fly fishing spots in Italy, share your catches and connect with anglers. Flywaters is the no-kill community for fly fishing enthusiasts.

mapGate.title: EXPLORE ITALY'S BEST SPOTS
mapGate.description: To protect our rivers and share precise coordinates, Flywaters is a private community. Register for free to unlock the full map, fly suggestions, and real-time water levels.
